s2.q: The uterine sound has which of the following characteristics?
s2.explain: The uterine sound is malleable, has a blunt tip (3-4 mm diameter), is graduated, and curved. It is used to measure cervical and uterine length.

s2.q: Which is a complication of using a uterine sound?
s2.explain: Complications of uterine sound include shock and perforation of the uterus.

s3.q: The female uterine catheter is used for:
options: Emptying the bladder during gynecologic operations
s3.explain: The female uterine catheter (available in rubber, metal, or plastic) is used for emptying the bladder during gynecologic operations.

s4.q: Which of the following is a type of cervical dilator?
s4.explain: Cervical dilators include Hegar (graduated dilators) and Fenton (alternative design). They are used for dilatation of the cervix to facilitate intrauterine surgery.

s4.q: Complications of cervical dilators include all EXCEPT:
s4.explain: Complications include neurogenic shock, infection, perforation, and cervical lacerations. Uterine prolapse is not a complication of cervical dilatation.

s5.q: The uterine curette is indicated for all EXCEPT:
s5.explain: The uterine curette (blunt or sharp) is used for evacuation of the uterus in incomplete/missed abortion and abnormal uterine bleeding, not for delivery of a viable fetus.

s6.q: Which forceps is used to hold the edges of the uterine incision during cesarean section?
s6.explain: Green Armytage forceps hold the edges of the uterine incision in cesarean section. Ring forceps have a lock and grasp the cervix during cerclage/curettage.

s6.q: What distinguishes ovum forceps from ring forceps?
options: Ovum forceps has a lock; ring forceps does not
Ring forceps has a lock; ovum forceps does not
Ovum forceps is used for CS
s6.explain: Ring forceps has a lock while ovum forceps has no lock. Both are used to remove products of conception.

options: Single or multiple teeth for grasping cervix
A lock and a long shank
s6.explain: Vulsellum has single-toothed or multi-toothed tips for holding the cervix in different operations.

s7.q: Sims vaginal speculum is used for:
options: Vaginal operations and diagnosis of VVF
s7.explain: Sims speculum is used for vaginal operations and diagnosis of vesicovaginal fistula (VVF). Cusco (bivalve) speculum is used for cervical visualization, IUD removal, Pap smear, and punch biopsy.

s7.q: Cusco speculum is used for all of the following EXCEPT:
s7.explain: Cusco (bivalve) speculum is used for cervical visualization in vaginal procedures, IUD removal, Pap smear, and cervical punch biopsy. Sims speculum is used for vaginal operations and VVF diagnosis.
